Bash(python3 << 'EOF'
import re

def _normalize_text\(text\):
    text = text.replace\("\\r\\n", "\\n"\).replace\("\\r", "\\n"\)
    text = re.sub\(r"\\n+", " ", text\)
    text = re.sub\(r" {2,}", " ", text\)
    return text.strip\(\)

def chunk_text\(text, max_chars=300\):
    text = _normalize_text\(text\)
    if not text:
        return []
    sentences = re.split\(r"\(?<=[.!?]\)\\s+", text\)
    chunks = []
    current = []
    current_len = 0
    for s in sentences:
        s_len = len\(s\) + 1
        if current_len + s_len > max_chars and current:
            chunks.append\(" ".join\(current\)\)
            current = []
            current_len = 0
        current.append\(s\)
        current_len += s_len
    if current:
        chunks.append\(" ".join\(current\)\)
    return chunks

# Paste the raw text from the job \(first ~2000 chars we saw\)
sample = r"""Date: June 10, 2025 Chapter 1 - Yazd, Aliyah to Israel, Kiryat Malachi The audio file below is a dub of Chapter 1 below also has audio clips for each section individually, so anyone can listen Or call Ham his priorityHebrew

English
Family treeRoots


I was born in the city of Yazd, Iran, on Lag BaOmer, 18 Iyar 576, May 19, 1946. That's why they call me Shimon for
Name of Rabbi Shimon bar Yochai I didn't know the great-grandparents, but stories about them passed down to us through The parents, grandmothers, sons, aunts and uncles. Pictures from that generation have little as We will see later On my father David's side, there are no pictures or stories about the great-grandparents. We also don't have any pictures of Grandpa Eliyahu from my father's side On my mother's side there is a picture of great-grandmother Morwari and a picture of great-grandfather Hezekiah, as seen Later


________________

Great-grandmother Morwari

My mother's maternal grandmother, named Morwari \(Precious Stone\), was a special character with a special story
Her husband was named Eleazar. For the story of Morveri see Link below the image
On the special story of Morveri, click on the link Morwari and Elazar

________________

Great-grandfather Hezekiah

 My great-grandfather on my mother's father's side was Hezekiah
His wives' names are unknown

From left to right: Great-grandfather Hezekiah, his son Moshe from his second wife, grandfather David My great-grandfather Hezekiah had 5 children with his first wife: David, Aaron, Miriam, Sarah and more A son unknown."""

chunks = chunk_text\(sample\)
for i, c in enumerate\(chunks\):
    print\(f"\\n--- CHUNK {i+1} \({len\(c\)} chars\) ---"\)
    print\(repr\(c\)\)
EOF)
